skill.name: ckm:brand
skill.description: Brand voice, visual identity, messaging frameworks, asset management, brand consistency. Activate for branded content, tone of voice, marketing assets, brand compliance, style guides.
skill.summary: Complete brand management toolkit for defining voice, visual identity, messaging, and maintaining consistency across all marketing assets.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 418 potential issues, but all are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are triggered by markdown code fences (```) in documentation files, not actual code execution. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings match hex color codes (e.g. #3B82F6) and the word 'signature' in normal brand context. The four Node.js scripts perform legitimate brand management operations: reading brand guidelines, extracting/validating colors, and syncing design tokens. No malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: execSync in sync-brand-to-tokens.cjs
medium_findings.description: Uses child_process.execSync to invoke a local Node.js script for CSS regeneration. The command uses hardcoded paths from constants (DESIGN_TOKENS_JSON, DESIGN_TOKENS_CSS) and the GENERATE_TOKENS_SCRIPT is a relative path within the project. No user input is interpolated into the command string, making command injection unlikely.

low_findings.title: Filesystem write operations in scripts
low_findings.description: Scripts read and write local files (brand guidelines, design tokens, manifests). All operations are scoped to the project working directory and use path.join with process.cwd(). No path traversal risk observed.

content.user_title: Build and maintain consistent brand identity
content.value_statement: Maintaining brand consistency across content, assets, and design systems is hard when guidelines live in scattered documents. This skill provides structured frameworks, automated validation scripts, and design token synchronization to keep your brand voice and visuals aligned.
actual_capabilities: Define and document brand voice, personality, and tone with structured frameworks
Manage color palettes by extracting hex codes from brand guidelines markdown files
Validate marketing assets against naming conventions, size limits, and format requirements
Sync brand guidelines to design tokens in JSON and CSS variable formats
Extract brand context for injection into AI prompts for consistent content generation
limitations: Image color extraction requires external tools like ImageMagick or the ai-multimodal skill
Asset validation does not perform visual content analysis, only metadata checks
Design token generation depends on a separate design-system skill script being present

use_cases.title: Launch a new brand identity
use_cases.description: Use the starter template and guided workflow to define voice, colors, and typography from scratch.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing managers launching a new product or company

use_cases.title: Audit brand consistency across assets
use_cases.description: Validate existing marketing assets against naming rules, size limits, and palette compliance.
use_cases.target_user: Brand managers conducting quarterly compliance reviews

use_cases.title: Sync brand updates to design system
use_cases.description: Update brand guidelines and automatically propagate changes to design tokens and CSS variables.
use_cases.target_user: Design system engineers maintaining token infrastructure

prompt_templates.title: Create brand voice framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define a brand voice for a B2B SaaS company. Use the voice-framework to establish personality traits, tone guidelines, and prohibited terms.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new brand voice definition

prompt_templates.title: Extract brand context for content
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the inject-brand-context script and use the output to guide the blog post I am about to write about product launch.

prompt_templates.title: Validate marketing assets
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate all banner assets in the assets/banners directory against our current brand guidelines and report any issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Running a compliance audit on existing assets

prompt_templates.title: Update brand colors and sync tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the primary color to a new ocean blue (#1E40AF) and run the sync script to update all design tokens and CSS variables.
prompt_templates.scenario: Changing brand colors and propagating to design system

output_examples.input: Run inject-brand-context to show current brand settings
output: Apply these brand guidelines to all generated content.

best_practices: Keep brand guidelines in a single markdown file as the source of truth, then sync to design tokens
Run asset validation before publishing any marketing materials to catch naming and size issues early
Use the inject-brand-context output as a system prompt prefix when generating branded content
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode brand colors in code; always reference the design tokens generated from the guidelines
Do not skip the sync step after editing brand guidelines; this causes drift between docs and tokens
Do not use the brand skill for content that intentionally deviates from the brand voice without explicit override

faq.question: What file does the brand skill read as the source of truth?
faq.answer: The default source is docs/brand-guidelines.md. You can specify a different path as an argument to the scripts.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without the design-system skill?
faq.answer: Yes, but CSS variable regeneration requires the design-system generate-tokens script. The JSON token sync works independently.

faq.question: How do I add a new color to the palette?
faq.answer: Edit the Primary, Secondary, or Accent section in the brand guidelines markdown, then run the sync script to propagate changes.

faq.question: Does the asset validator check image content?
faq.answer: No, it checks metadata only: filename pattern, file size, format, and manifest registration. Use extract-colors for color compliance.

faq.question: What image formats are supported by the asset validator?
faq.answer: PNG, JPG, JPEG, WebP, GIF for images; SVG for vector; MP4, MOV, WebM for video; PDF, PSD, AI, FIG for documents.

faq.question: How do I extract colors from an image?
faq.answer: Use the extract-colors script with an image path. For actual color analysis, it provides commands for ImageMagick or the ai-multimodal skill.
